summary:
Toluene primarily impairs spatial learning through disruptions in neurotransmitter systems such as NMDA and GABA, not due to T-cell deficiency. Current evidence shows no mechanism or study supporting a link between T-cell deficiency and worsened spatial learning after toluene exposure.  

The summary above contains only two sentences focused solely on clinically significant findings related directly to the query about T-cell deficiency impacting spatial learning post-toluene exposure.
